name: Brain Stem
location: Base of brain connecting cerebrum and diencephalon to the spinal cord
description: Vital life-support stalk comprising Midbrain, Pons, and Medulla Oblongata, housing autonomic cardiac/respiratory centers and cranial nerve nuclei.
functions: Autonomic vital cardiovascular & respiratory rhythm control
Ascending Reticular Activating System (ARAS) consciousness maintenance
Cranial nerve III through XII nuclei origin and reflex integration
Ascending sensory and descending motor tract conduits
bloodSupply: Vertebrobasilar arterial system (Basilar Artery & Vertebral Arteries)
clinicalImportance: Herniation or focal lesions cause rapid loss of consciousness, cranial nerve palsies, or respiratory arrest.
researchNotes: Locus coeruleus in upper brainstem supplies norepinephrine across the entire cerebral mantle.
interestingFacts: All sensory and motor nerve signals between the brain and body bottleneck through this narrow 3-inch stem.
